jokes.text: Mac, you have the tendon dexterity of a sow
jokes.setup: Dennis criticizing Mac's stretching performance
jokes.punchline: Comparing his flexibility to that of a female pig
jokes.explanation: Specific, character-appropriate insult that establishes Dennis's condescending nature perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Mac, you have the tendon dexterity of a sow

jokes.text: Needy and desperate for approval, but, uh, good.
jokes.setup: Mac enthusiastically performs the stretches correctly
jokes.punchline: Dennis backhanded compliment revealing his narcissism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis character moment - can't give a compliment without an insult

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Needy and desperate for approval, but, uh, good.

jokes.text: With one little gesture, he tells me everything I need to know. That wordless display of confidence earns my confidence.
jokes.setup: Charlie makes a simple hand gesture
jokes.punchline: Dennis over-analyzing it as profound communication
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Dennis's pretentious over-interpretation of simple actions

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: With one little gesture, he tells me everything I need to know. That wordless display of confidence earns my confidence.

jokes.text: Don't say 'oyster.' Say 'clam.'
jokes.setup: Dennis uses the common phrase 'world is our oyster'
jokes.punchline: Charlie's arbitrary preference for clams over oysters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - completely arbitrary but delivered with conviction

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Don't say 'oyster.' Say 'clam.'

jokes.text: The extended shellfish debate that derails the entire conversation
jokes.setup: Simple correction about oysters vs clams
jokes.punchline: Spirals into detailed discussion of barnacles, abalone, mussels, and seagulls
jokes.explanation: Classic gang behavior - getting completely sidetracked by meaningless details

jokes.text: ♪ Uh, Dee is in the house ♪
jokes.setup: Serious planning moment interrupted
jokes.punchline: Dee's awful improvised entrance song
jokes.explanation: Typical Dee - oblivious to how annoying she is

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: ♪ Uh, Dee is in the house ♪

jokes.text: First things first, we got to ditch Dee.
jokes.setup: Dee announces her presence enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Immediate plot to abandon her
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff structure and completely in character

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: First things first, we got to ditch Dee.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You forgot. Oh, that makes it so much better.
jokes.setup: The gang clearly has no idea what she's talking about
jokes.punchline: Dee's delight at their forgetfulness making her reveal more satisfying
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee's petty satisfaction

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, my God. You forgot. Oh, that makes it so much better.

jokes.text: Dee's a bird! You're a bird! I hate you, bird!
jokes.setup: Final seconds before Dee Day begins
jokes.punchline: Frantic attempts to get their bird insults in
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and desperation, classic running gag

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Dee's a bird!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You're a bird!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I hate you, bird!

jokes.text: Today, it's you who will be forced to sit and just listen. And my first topic? My feelings.
jokes.setup: Dee taking control and promising revenge
jokes.punchline: Her 'punishment' is making them listen to her feelings
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the torture to come, very Dee-like approach

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Today, it's you who will be forced to sit and just listen. And my first topic? My feelings.

jokes.text: Today of all days, and now I'm being forced to listen to her feelings? I won't do it. And who cares about her feelings anyway? Nobody, that's who.
jokes.setup: Dee starts sharing feelings
jokes.punchline: Dennis's immediate internal revolt and narcissistic dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - completely self-centered and dismissive

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Today of all days, and now I'm being forced to listen to her feelings? I won't do it. And who cares about her feelings anyway? Nobody, that's who.

jokes.text: Uh-oh. I'm forgetting my part of the plan with every second that goes by. Dennis is gonna yell at me again.
jokes.setup: Everyone's internal monologues during Dee's feelings speech
jokes.punchline: Mac's anxiety about disappointing Dennis
jokes.explanation: Good character insight into Mac's desperate need for approval

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Uh-oh. I'm forgetting my part of the plan with every second that goes by. Dennis is gonna yell at me again.

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure Dennis has candy. I heard a crinkle in his pocket that sounded like the wrapper of a candy.
jokes.setup: Charlie's internal monologue during feelings time
jokes.punchline: His laser focus on potential candy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie - completely distracted by simple pleasures

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm pretty sure Dennis has candy. I heard a crinkle in his pocket that sounded like the wrapper of a candy.

jokes.text: Frank's extended clam preparation rant ending with 'Make the world your clam. Just sounds better.'
jokes.setup: Frank's internal monologue about shellfish
jokes.punchline: Elaborate justification for Charlie's earlier clam preference
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Frank's practical, crude thinking

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Make the world your clam. Just sounds better.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah. Absolutely. Everything you were saying, we were totally listening.
jokes.setup: Long sequence of them clearly not listening
jokes.punchline: Obviously fake agreement when she asks if they were listening
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective payoff to the not-listening sequence

dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, yeah. Absolutely. Everything you were saying, we were totally listening.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go take a quick squirt 'cause that beer just went right through me
jokes.setup: Polite transition from her feelings speech
jokes.punchline: Crude language choice that undermines any sophistication
jokes.explanation: Typical Dee - trying to be refined but immediately crude

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm gonna go take a quick squirt 'cause that beer just went right through me

jokes.text: She just went to the bathroom. See, everybody's got to take a dump at least once in every 24-hour period.
jokes.setup: Need for a new plan
jokes.punchline: Treating bathroom breaks as tactical opportunities with pseudo-scientific reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis logic - treating biological functions as strategy

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: She just went to the bathroom. See, everybody's got to take a dump at least once in every 24-hour period.

jokes.text: What? No, that's not what he's talking about, Mac. What he means is that it's time for me to go to the school, break in using my old janitor's key...
jokes.setup: Mac thinks Charlie literally needs to poop
jokes.punchline: Charlie's elaborate explanation of his actual criminal mission
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and shows Charlie's criminal competence

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What? No, that's not what he's talking about, Mac. What he means is that it's time for me to go to the school, break in using my old janitor's key...

jokes.text: And keep this kind of good focus in mind when it comes to what's inside your pants, 'cause I want that, man. I want what's in your pants.
jokes.setup: Dennis praising Charlie's focus
jokes.punchline: Accidentally homoerotic phrasing about wanting Charlie's candy
jokes.explanation: Good accidental innuendo that Dennis immediately tries to correct

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And keep this kind of good focus in mind when it comes to what's inside your pants, 'cause I want that, man. I want what's in your pants.

jokes.text: I got fired on account of a relationship I was having with a Juggalo child
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining his past employment
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of inappropriate relationship with disturbing specificity
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark and specific, perfectly delivered as casual information

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I got fired on account of a relationship I was having with a Juggalo child

jokes.text: I'm ready to have new relationships with the children.
jokes.setup: Already revealed inappropriate past relationship
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he wants to start more inappropriate relationships
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that makes the first joke even worse in hindsight

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm ready to have new relationships with the children.

jokes.text: They were breaking on in to your bicycle. Kicked it in half. Filled it with horse manure.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to get Terry to leave by claiming vandalism
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous details about bicycle destruction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd details, typical Charlie chaos

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: They were breaking on in to your bicycle. Kicked it in half. Filled it with horse manure.

jokes.text: Uh, I'm gonna fuck your bike up, Terry.
jokes.setup: All of Charlie's manipulation attempts have failed
jokes.punchline: Direct threat delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from manipulation to direct threat, delivered with Charlie's casual insanity

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Uh, I'm gonna fuck your bike up, Terry.

jokes.text: It's insanely racist. We didn't want to do it, but you know, Dee's humor is so lowbrow.
jokes.setup: Mac in racist costume complaining about racism
jokes.punchline: Blaming Dee for making them do racist characters while actively doing them
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on their own terrible behavior

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's insanely racist. We didn't want to do it, but you know, Dee's humor is so lowbrow.

jokes.text: That's why you suck at everything, Maxie. I say if you're gonna do a racist stereotype, you best do it right.
jokes.setup: Frank still in character criticizing Mac
jokes.punchline: Advocating for better racism while calling Mac by diminutive name
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Frank logic - if you're going to be terrible, be thoroughly terrible

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's why you suck at everything, Maxie. I say if you're gonna do a racist stereotype, you best do it right.

jokes.text: I want you to ram him, all right? Nothing crazy, but it's got to be believable.
jokes.setup: Need to create car accident distraction
jokes.punchline: Casual planning of vehicular assault with qualification about believability
jokes.explanation: Good casual criminality with Mac's attempt at professionalism

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I want you to ram him, all right? Nothing crazy, but it's got to be believable.

jokes.text: It won't be the clams, because if it was clams... ...clams and I was sick, it would be all over the pavement.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to be professional valet while obviously sick
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about potential vomiting while callback to clam debate
jokes.explanation: Good callback and gross-out humor, Frank trying to be reassuring while being disturbing

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It won't be the clams, because if it was clams... ...clams and I was sick, it would be all over the pavement.

jokes.text: It's not the clams! / Frank immediately vomits
jokes.setup: Frank insisting it's not the clams making him sick
jokes.punchline: Immediately vomiting, proving it was the clams
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff with immediate contradiction through physical comedy

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's not the clams!
Frank immediately vomits

jokes.text: It's not the clams!
jokes.setup: Frank has thrown up all over the councilwoman's car
jokes.punchline: Still insisting it wasn't the clams while covered in vomit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the clam callback, Frank's stubborn denial in face of evidence

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's not the clams!

jokes.text: 'with the quivering lips of two souls desperate to taste the juices of passion.' Did that seem like that to you guys?
jokes.setup: Dennis and Charlie just had an awkward stage kiss
jokes.punchline: Dee reading overwrought stage directions and critiquing their performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee moment - making already uncomfortable situation worse with bad writing

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: 'with the quivering lips of two souls desperate to taste the juices of passion.' Did that seem like that to you guys?

jokes.text: Plant one on me, Covington! / Forced romantic scene between Mac and Charlie
jokes.setup: Dee directing her offensive play
jokes.punchline: Mac and Charlie having to kiss as part of Dee's terrible characters
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy forcing the characters into uncomfortable romantic situation

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Plant one on me, Covington!
Forced romantic scene between Mac and Charlie

jokes.text: with the quivering lips of two souls desperate to taste the juices of passion
jokes.setup: Dee reads stage directions for the kiss scene
jokes.punchline: The stage directions are hilariously overwrought and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful romantic writing that captures Dee's terrible artistic sensibilities.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: with the quivering lips of two souls desperate to taste the juices of passion

jokes.text: And I know we're supposed to be cool with this, 'cause, like, that's where society's headed, but I'm just... I'm not there.
jokes.setup: Having to kiss Dennis for the play
jokes.punchline: Mac's confused attempt to be progressive while expressing homophobia
jokes.explanation: Great Mac character moment - trying to sound enlightened while being completely backwards

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: And I know we're supposed to be cool with this, 'cause, like, that's where society's headed, but I'm just... I'm not there.

jokes.text: Well, now you know how it feels. Oh, I don't want to taste your cheese.
jokes.setup: Aftermath of their forced kiss
jokes.punchline: Discussing lingering tastes from each other's mouths
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that extends the awkwardness, moderate impact

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, now you know how it feels.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, I don't want to taste your cheese.

jokes.text: The ostrich's eyes are bigger than its brain. Do you have any thoughts on that?
jokes.setup: Dee sharing bird facts during forced bird-watching
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently describing herself while asking for their opinion
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic self-description through bird facts, perfect setup

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: The ostrich's eyes are bigger than its brain. Do you have any thoughts on that?

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I want to scream 'bird,' I want to scream 'bird.' That would be so good.
jokes.setup: Forced to listen to bird facts without calling Dee a bird
jokes.punchline: Charlie's desperate desire to say the forbidden word
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their frustration, Charlie's childlike impulse control issues

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, my God, I want to scream 'bird,' I want to scream 'bird.' That would be so good.

jokes.text: Charlie has been outshining me all day, and I want to shine for you, Dennis
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his poor performance
jokes.punchline: Revealing his desperate need for Dennis's approval
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - pathetic need for validation

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie has been outshining me all day, and I want to shine for you, Dennis

jokes.text: I haven't been outshining you. I was just throwing you looks because I wanted the candy, but I was gonna share it with you
jokes.setup: Mac feeling threatened by Charlie's performance
jokes.punchline: Charlie revealing his simple candy-motivated behavior and willingness to share
jokes.explanation: Sweet Charlie moment showing his simple motivations and genuine kindness

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I haven't been outshining you. I was just throwing you looks because I wanted the candy, but I was gonna share it with you

jokes.text: I keep candy in my pocket sometimes so that I can get Charlie's focus. It was working like a charm, man.
jokes.setup: Revelation that Dennis has candy
jokes.punchline: Treating Charlie like a pet or child with candy rewards
jokes.explanation: Great reveal of Dennis's manipulative tactics and view of Charlie as subhuman

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I keep candy in my pocket sometimes so that I can get Charlie's focus. It was working like a charm, man.

jokes.text: I'll bang her as fast as I possibly can, and then, while she's cleaning up afterwards, I'll set all of her clocks back
jokes.setup: Dennis's backup plan to seduce the councilwoman
jokes.punchline: Coldly mechanical approach to sex as just another step in the plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis sociopathy - treating sex as purely transactional

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'll bang her as fast as I possibly can, and then, while she's cleaning up afterwards, I'll set all of her clocks back

jokes.text: I'm talking about the concealer, yes. But I'm talking about the rest of the makeup, the tape you have behind your jaw and your eyes. I'm talking about the hair paint.
jokes.setup: Dee making Dennis remove his 'makeup'
jokes.punchline: Revealing the extensive cosmetic enhancements Dennis uses
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation about Dennis's vanity and insecurity

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm talking about the concealer, yes. But I'm talking about the rest of the makeup, the tape you have behind your jaw and your eyes. I'm talking about the hair paint.

jokes.text: That's what his soul looks like.
jokes.setup: Dennis without makeup approaching the councilwoman
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact assessment of Dennis's true appearance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag payoff with perfect economy and philosophical depth

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's what his soul looks like.

jokes.text: Do you like hard candy? I have candies. Oh, you know what? Actually, I don't. Scratch that. I threw all the hard candies at those idiot children.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to start conversation with councilwoman
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting to throwing candy at children
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Dennis completely falling apart

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Do you like hard candy? I have candies. Oh, you know what? Actually, I don't. Scratch that. I threw all the hard candies at those idiot children.

jokes.text: Can I get your house key? We don't have to have sex like this. I can put another man's face on.
jokes.setup: Dennis's pickup attempt getting more desperate
jokes.punchline: Offering to wear someone else's face for sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing escalation, perfectly delivered casual insanity

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Can I get your house key? We don't have to have sex like this. I can put another man's face on.

jokes.text: This man is clearly a monster, and he will be punished accordingly.
jokes.setup: Dennis's horrific pickup attempt
jokes.punchline: Dee correctly identifying Dennis as a monster for once
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where Dee is actually right about Dennis

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: This man is clearly a monster, and he will be punished accordingly.

jokes.text: In a stunning upset, local ordinance LC-103 passed today. The controversial proposal actually legalizes public urination.
jokes.setup: Professional news delivery about local politics
jokes.punchline: The ordinance is about legalizing public urination
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist premise delivered with professional gravity

dialogue.speaker: News anchor
dialogue.line: In a stunning upset, local ordinance LC-103 passed today. The controversial proposal actually legalizes public urination.

jokes.text: Why didn't you just go and slash her tires, like, five minutes before she had to leave for the meeting?
jokes.setup: The gang's elaborate failed plan
jokes.punchline: Dee immediately suggesting a much simpler criminal solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncturing of their overcomplicated scheme with obvious solution

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Why didn't you just go and slash her tires, like, five minutes before she had to leave for the meeting?

jokes.text: My plan did work, jackass. I got close enough to the councilwoman in the restaurant to steal her wallet, got her address, and then replaced the wallet without her seeing
jokes.setup: Dee being dismissed as incompetent
jokes.punchline: Revealing she successfully executed her own plan while they failed
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing Dee was competent all along while they were failing

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: My plan did work, jackass. I got close enough to the councilwoman in the restaurant to steal her wallet, got her address, and then replaced the wallet without her seeing

jokes.text: Did you see how many times I peed today? I have a teeny, tiny, little bladder. I want to be able to pee on the street if I have to.
jokes.setup: Revealing why she helped with the ordinance
jokes.punchline: Dee's personal motivation and admission of small bladder
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation and callback to her bathroom behavior

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Did you see how many times I peed today? I have a teeny, tiny, little bladder. I want to be able to pee on the street if I have to.

jokes.text: You should all probably check your phones. Just 'cause I pushed the cuckoo clock ahead an hour
jokes.setup: The guys celebrating their victory and insulting Dee
jokes.punchline: Dee revealing she manipulated time to extend Dee Day
jokes.explanation: Brilliant final twist completely reversing the power dynamic and extending their suffering

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You should all probably check your phones. Just 'cause I pushed the cuckoo clock ahead an hour

jokes.text: You just insulted me on Dee Day. You know what that earns you? Another day of Dee.
jokes.setup: The gang celebrating and calling her a bird again
jokes.punchline: Revealing she manipulated them into extending Dee Day
jokes.explanation: Perfect final twist that recontextualizes the entire episode - Dee was in control all along

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You just insulted me on Dee Day. You know what that earns you? Another day of Dee.
